(written < sizeInBytes())\n    88→            written += channel.write(buffer);\n    89→        buffer.reset();\n    90→        return written;\n    91→    }\n    92→\n    93→    /**\n    94→     * The total number of bytes in this message set not including any partial, trailing messages. This\n    95→     * may be smaller than what is returned by {@link #sizeInBytes()}.\n    96→     * @return The number of valid bytes\n    97→     */\n    98→    public int validBytes() {\n    99→        if (validBytes >= 0)\n   100→            return validBytes;\n   101→\n   102→        int bytes = 0;\n   103→        for (RecordBatch batch : batches())\n   104→            bytes += batch.sizeInBytes();\n   105→\n   106→        this.validBytes = bytes;\n   107→        return bytes;\n   108→    }\n   109→\n   110→    @Override\n   111→    public AbstractIterator<MutableRecordBatch> batchIterator() {\n   112→        return new RecordBatchIterator<>(new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer.duplicate(), Integer.MAX_VALUE));\n   113→    }\n   114→\n   115→    /**\n   116→     * Validates the header of the first batch and returns batch size.\n   117→     * @return first batch size including LOG_OVERHEAD if buffer contains header up to\n   118→     *         magic byte, null otherwise\n   119→     * @throws CorruptRecordException if record size or magic is invalid\n   120→     */\n   121→    public Integer firstBatchSize() {\n   122→        if (buffer.remaining() < HEADER_SIZE_UP_TO_MAGIC)\n   123→            return null;\n   124→        return new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer, Integer.MAX_VALUE).nextBatchSize();\n   125→    }\n   126→\n   127→    /**\n   128→     * Filter the records into the provided ByteBuffer.\n   129→     *\n   130→     * @param filter                      The filter function\n   131→     * @param destinationBuffer           The byte buffer to write the filtered records to\n   132→     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported. For small\n   133→     *                                    record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4) will\n   134→     *                                    dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the\n   135→     *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant\n   136→     *                                    performance impact.\n   137→     * @return A FilterResult with a summary of the output (for metrics) and potentially an overflow buffer\n   138→     */\n   139→    public FilterResult filterTo(RecordFilter filter, ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n   140→        return filterTo(batches(), filter, destinationBuffer, decompressionBufferSupplier);\n   141→    }\n   142→\n   143→    /**\n   144→     * Note: This method is also used to convert the first timestamp of the batch (which is usually the timestamp of the first record)\n   145→     * to the delete horizon of the tombstones or txn markers which are present in the batch.\n   146→     */\n   147→    private static FilterResult filterTo(Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches, RecordFilter filter,\n   148→                                         ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n   149→        FilterResult filterResult = new FilterResult(destinationBuffer);\n   150→        ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(destinationBuffer);\n   151→        for (MutableRecordBatch batch : batches) {\n   152→            final BatchRetentionResult batchRetentionResult = filter.checkBatchRetention(batch);\n   153→            final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = batchRetentionResult.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n   154→            final BatchRetention batchRetention = batchRetentionResult.batchRetention;\n   155→\n   156→            filterResult.bytesRead += batch.sizeInBytes();\n   157→\n   158→            if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.DELETE)\n   159→                continue;\n   160→\n   161→            final BatchFilterResult iterationResult = filterBatch(batch, decompressionBufferSupplier, filterResult,\n   162→                filter);\n   163→            List<Record> retainedRecords = iterationResult.retainedRecords;\n   164→            boolean containsTombstones = iterationResult.containsTombstones;\n   165→            boolean writeOriginalBatch = iterationResult.writeOriginalBatch;\n   166→            long maxOffset = iterationResult.maxOffset;\n   167→\n   168→            if (!retainedRecords.isEmpty()) {\n   169→                // we check if the delete horizon should be set to a new value\n   170→                // in which case, we need to reset the base timestamp and overwrite the timestamp deltas\n   171→                // if the batch does not contain tombstones, then we don't need to overwrite batch\n   172→                boolean needToSetDeleteHorizon = (containsTombstones || containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) &&\n   173→                    batch.deleteHorizonMs().isEmpty();\n   174→                if (writeOriginalBatch && !needToSetDeleteHorizon) {\n   175→                    batch.writeTo(bufferOutputStream);\n   176→                    filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, retainedRecords.size(), false);\n   177→                } else {\n   178→                    long deleteHorizonMs;\n   179→                    if (needToSetDeleteHorizon)\n   180→                        deleteHorizonMs = filter.currentTime + filter.deleteRetentionMs;\n   181→                    else\n   182→                        deleteHorizonMs = batch.deleteHorizonMs().orElse(RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP);\n   183→                    try (final MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = buildRetainedRecordsInto(batch, retainedRecords,\n   184→                            bufferOutputStream, deleteHorizonMs)) {\n   185→                        MemoryRecords records = builder.build();\n   186→                        int filteredBatchSize = records.sizeInBytes();\n   187→                        MemoryRecordsBuilder.RecordsInfo info = builder.info();\n   188→                        filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(info.maxTimestamp, info.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp,\n   189→                            maxOffset, retainedRecords.size(), filteredBatchSize);\n   190→                    }\n   191→                }\n   192→            } else if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.RETAIN_EMPTY) {\n   193→                if (batch.magic() < RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2) // should never happen\n   194→                    throw new IllegalStateException(\"Empty batches are only supported for magic v2 and above\");\n   195→\n   196→                bufferOutputStream.ensureRemaining(DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD);\n   197→                DefaultRecordBatch.writeEmptyHeader(bufferOutputStream.buffer(), RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, batch.producerId(),\n   198→                        batch.producerEpoch(), batch.baseSequence(), batch.baseOffset(), batch.lastOffset(),\n   199→                        batch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), batch.timestampType(), batch.maxTimestamp(),\n   200→                        batch.isTransactional(), batch.isControlBatch());\n   201→                filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, 0, true);\n   202→            }\n   203→\n   204→            // If we had to allocate a new buffer to fit the filtered buffer (see KAFKA-5316), return early to\n   205→            // avoid the need for additional allocations.\n   206→            ByteBuffer outputBuffer = bufferOutputStream.buffer();\n   207→            if (outputBuffer != destinationBuffer) {\n   208→                filterResult.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n   209→                return filterResult;\n   210→            }\n   211→        }\n   212→\n   213→        return filterResult;\n   214→    }\n   215→\n   216→    private static BatchFilterResult filterBatch(RecordBatch batch,\n   217→                                                 BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier,\n   218→                                                 FilterResult filterResult,\n   219→                                                 RecordFilter filter) {\n   220→        try (final CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = batch.streamingIterator(decompressionBufferSupplier)) {\n   221→            long maxOffset = -1;\n   222→            boolean containsTombstones = false;\n   223→            // Convert records with old record versions\n   224→            boolean writeOriginalBatch = batch.magic() >= RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE;\n   225→            List<Record> retainedRecords = new ArrayList<>();\n   226→            while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n   227→                Record record = iterator.next();\n   228→                filterResult.messagesRead += 1;\n   229→\n   230→                if (filter.shouldRetainRecord(batch, record)) {\n   231→                    if (record.offset() > maxOffset)\n   232→                        maxOffset = record.offset();\n   233→\n   234→                    retainedRecords.add(record);\n   235→\n   236→                    if (!record.hasValue()) {\n   237→                        containsTombstones = true;\n   238→                    }\n   239→                } else {\n   240→                    writeOriginalBatch = false;\n   241→                }\n   242→            }\n   243→            return new BatchFilterResult(retainedRecords, writeOriginalBatch, containsTombstones, maxOffset);\n   244→        }\n   245→    }\n   246→\n   247→    private static class BatchFilterResult {\n   248→        private final List<Record> retainedRecords;\n   249→        private final boolean writeOriginalBatch;\n   250→        private final boolean containsTombstones;\n   251→        private final long maxOffset;\n   252→        private BatchFilterResult(List<Record> retainedRecords,\n   253→                                  final boolean writeOriginalBatch,\n   254→                                  final boolean containsTombstones,\n   255→                                  final long maxOffset) {\n   256→            this.retainedRecords = retainedRecords;\n   257→            this.writeOriginalBatch = writeOriginalBatch;\n   258→            this.containsTombstones = containsTombstones;\n   259→            this.maxOffset = maxOffset;\n   260→        }\n   261→    }\n   262→\n   263→    private static MemoryRecordsBuilder buildRetainedRecordsInto(RecordBatch originalBatch,\n   264→                                                                 List<Record> retainedRecords,\n   265→                                                                 ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream,\n   266→                                                                 final long deleteHorizonMs) {\n   267→        Compression compression = Compression.of(originalBatch.compressionType()).build();\n   268→        // V0 has no timestamp type or timestamp, so we set the timestamp to CREATE_TIME and timestamp to NO_TIMESTAMP.\n   269→        // Note that this differs from produce up-conversion where the timestamp type topic config is used and the log append\n   270→        // time is generated if the config is LOG_APPEND_TIME. The reason for the different behavior is that there is\n   271→        // no appropriate log append time we can generate at compaction time.\n   272→        TimestampType timestampType = originalBatch.timestampType() == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE ?\n   273→                TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : originalBatch.timestampType();\n   274→        long logAppendTime = timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ?\n   275→                originalBatch.maxTimestamp() : RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n   276→        long baseOffset = originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2 ?\n   277→                originalBatch.baseOffset() : retainedRecords.get(0).offset();\n   278→\n   279→        // Convert records with older record versions to the current one\n   280→        MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferOutputStream, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\n   281→                compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime, originalBatch.producerId(),\n   282→                originalBatch.producerEpoch(), originalBatch.baseSequence(), originalBatch.isTransactional(),\n   283→                originalBatch.isControlBatch(), originalBatch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), bufferOutputStream.limit(), deleteHorizonMs);\n   284→\n   285→        for (Record record : retainedRecords)\n   286→            builder.append(record);\n   287→\n   288→        if (originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2)\n   289→            // we must preserve the last offset from the initial batch in order to ensure that the\n   290→            // last sequence number from the batch remains even after compaction. Otherwise, the producer\n   291→            // could incorrectly see an out of sequence error.\n   292→            builder.overrideLastOffset(originalBatch.lastOffset());\n   293→\n   294→        return builder;\n   295→    }\n   296→\n   297→    /**\n   298→     * Get the byte buffer that backs this instance for reading.\n   299→     */\n   300→    public ByteBuffer buffer() {\n   301→        return buffer.duplicate();\n   302→    }\n   303→\n   304→    @Override\n   305→    public MemoryRecords slice(int position, int size) {\n   306→        if (position < 0)\n   307→            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid position: \" + position + \" in read from \" + this);\n   308→        if (position > buffer.limit())\n   309→            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Slice from position \" + position + \" exceeds end position of \" + this);\n   310→        if (size < 0)\n   311→            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid size: \" + size + \" in read from \" + this);\n   312→\n   313→        int availableBytes = Math.min(size, buffer.limit() - position);\n   314→        // As of now, clients module support Java11 hence can't use ByteBuffer::slice(position, size) method.\n   315→        // So we need to create a duplicate buffer and set the position and limit. Duplicate buffer\n   316→        // is backed by original bytes hence not the content but only the relative position and limit\n   317→        // are changed in the duplicate buffer. Once the position and limit are set, we can call the\n   318→        // slice method to get the sliced buffer, which is a backed by the original buffer with the\n   319→        // position reset to 0 and limit set to the size of the slice.\n   320→        ByteBuffer slicedBuffer = buffer.duplicate();\n   321→        slicedBuffer.position(position);\n   322→        slicedBuffer.limit(position + availableBytes);\n   323→        // Reset the position to 0 so that the sliced view has a relative position.\n   324→        slicedBuffer = slicedBuffer.slice();\n   325→\n   326→        return readableRecords(slicedBuffer);\n   327→    }\n   328→\n   329→    @Override\n   330→    public Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches() {\n   331→        return batches;\n   332→    }\n   333→\n   334→    @Override\n   335→    public String toString() {\n   336→        return \"MemoryRecords(size=\" + sizeInBytes() +\n   337→                \", buffer=\" + buffer +\n   338→                \")\";\n   339→    }\n   340→\n   341→    @Override\n   342→    public boolean equals(Object o) {\n   343→        if (this == o)\n   344→            return true;\n   345→        if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\n   346→            return false;\n   347→\n   348→        MemoryRecords that = (MemoryRecords) o;\n   349→\n   350→        return buffer.equals(that.buffer);\n   351→    }\n   352→\n   353→    @Override\n   354→    public int hashCode() {\n   355→        return buffer.hashCode();\n   356→    }\n   357→\n   358→    public abstract static class RecordFilter {\n   359→        public final long currentTime;\n   360→        public final long deleteRetentionMs;\n   361→\n   362→        public RecordFilter(final long currentTime, final long deleteRetentionMs) {\n   363→            this.currentTime = currentTime;\n   364→            this.deleteRetentionMs = deleteRetentionMs;\n   365→        }\n   366→\n   367→        public static class BatchRetentionResult {\n   368→            public final BatchRetention batchRetention;\n   369→            public final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n   370→            public BatchRetentionResult(final BatchRetention batchRetention,\n   371→                                        final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) {\n   372→                this.batchRetention = batchRetention;\n   373→                this.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n   374→            }\n   375→        }\n   376→\n   377→        public enum BatchRetention {\n   378→            DELETE, // Delete the batch without inspecting records\n   379→            RETAIN_EMPTY, // Retain the batch even if it is empty\n   380→            DELETE_EMPTY  // Delete the batch if it is empty\n   381→        }\n   382→\n   383→        /**\n   384→         * Check whether the full batch can be discarded (i.e. whether we even need to\n   385→         * check the records individually).\n   386→         */\n   387→        protected abstract BatchRetentionResult checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch batch);\n   388→\n   389→        /**\n   390→         * Check whether a record should be retained in the log. Note that {@link #checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)}\n   391→         * is used prior to checking individual record retention. Only records from batches which were not\n   392→         * explicitly discarded with {@link BatchRetention#DELETE} will be considered.\n   393→         */\n   394→        protected abstract boolean shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch recordBatch, Record record);\n   395→    }\n   396→\n   397→    public static class FilterResult {\n   398→        private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;\n   399→        private int messagesRead = 0;\n   400→        // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from\n   401→        // `messagesRead` and any discarded batches.\n   402→        private int bytesRead = 0;\n   403→        private int messagesRetained = 0;\n   404→        private int bytesRetained = 0;\n   405→        private long maxOffset = -1L;\n   406→        private long maxTimestamp = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n   407→        private long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = -1L;\n   408→\n   409→        private FilterResult(ByteBuffer outputBuffer) {\n   410→            this.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n   411→        }\n   412→\n   413→        private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(MutableRecordBatch retainedBatch, int numMessagesInBatch, boolean headerOnly) {\n   414→            int bytesRetained = headerOnly ? DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD : retainedBatch.sizeInBytes();\n   415→            updateRetainedBatchMetadata(retainedBatch.maxTimestamp(), retainedBatch.lastOffset(),\n   416→                    retainedBatch.lastOffset(), numMessagesInBatch, bytesRetained);\n   417→        }\n   418→\n   419→        private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset,\n   420→                                                 int messagesRetained, int bytesRetained) {\n   421→            validateBatchMetadata(maxTimestamp, shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, maxOffset);\n   422→            if (maxTimestamp > this.maxTimestamp) {\n   423→                this.maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp;\n   424→                this.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\n   425→            }\n   426→            this.maxOffset = Math.max(maxOffset, this.maxOffset);\n   427→            this.messagesRetained += messagesRetained;\n   428→            this.bytesRetained += bytesRetained;\n   429→        }\n   430→\n   431→        private void validateBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset) {\n   432→            if (maxTimestamp != RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP && shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp < 0)\n   433→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"shallowOffset undefined for maximum timestamp \" + maxTimestamp);\n   434→            if (maxOffset < 0)\n   435→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"maxOffset undefined\");\n   436→        }\n   437→\n   438→        public ByteBuffer outputBuffer() {\n   439→            return outputBuffer;\n   440→        }\n   441→\n   442→        public int messagesRead() {\n   443→            return messagesRead;\n   444→        }\n   445→\n   446→        public int bytesRead() {\n   447→            return bytesRead;\n   448→        }\n   449→\n   450→        public int messagesRetained() {\n   451→            return messagesRetained;\n   452→        }\n   453→\n   454→        public int bytesRetained() {\n   455→            return bytesRetained;\n   456→        }\n   457→\n   458→        public long maxOffset() {\n   459→            return maxOffset;\n   460→        }\n   461→\n   462→        public long maxTimestamp() {\n   463→            return maxTimestamp;\n   464→        }\n   465→\n   466→        public long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp() {\n   467→            return shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\n   468→        }\n   469→    }\n   470→\n   471→    public static MemoryRecords readableRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n   472→        return new MemoryRecords(buffer);\n   473→    }\n   474→\n   475→    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n   476→                                               Compression compression,\n   477→                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n   478→                                               long baseOffset) {\n   479→        return builder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset);\n   480→    }\n   481→\n   482→    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n   483→                                               Compression compression,\n   484→                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n   485→                                               long baseOffset,\n   486→                                               int maxSize) {\n   487→        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n   488→        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n   489→            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();\n   490→\n   491→        return new MemoryRecordsBuilder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset,\n   492→            logAppendTime, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE,\n   493→            false, false, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, maxSize);\n   494→    }\n   495→\n   496→    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n   497→                                               byte magic,\n   498→                                               Compression compression,\n   499→                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n   500→                                               long baseOffset,\n   501→                                               long logAppendTime) {\n   502→        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime,\n   503→                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, false,\n   504→                RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n   505→    }\n   506→\n   507→    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n   508→                                               byte magic,\n   509→                                               Compression compression,\n   510→                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n   511→                                               long baseOffset) {\n   512→        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n   513→        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n   514→            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();\n   515→        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime,\n   516→                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, false,\n   517→                RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n   518→    }\n   519→\n   520→    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n   521→                                               byte magic,\n   522→                                               Compression compression,\n   523→                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n   524→                                               long baseOffset,\n   525→                                               long logAppendTime,\n   526→                                               int partitionLeaderEpoch) {\n   527→        return builder(buffer, magic, compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime,\n   528→                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, false, partitionLeaderEpoch);\n   529→    }\n   530→\n   531→    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n   532→                                               Compression compression,\n   533→                                               long baseOffset,\n   534→                                               long producerId,\n   535→                                               short producerEpoch,\n   536→                                               int baseSequence,\n   537→                                               boolean isTransactional) {\n   538→        return builder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, baseOffset,\n   539→                RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP, producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional,\n   540→                RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n   541→    }\n   542→\n   543→    public static       long logAppendTime,\n   549→                                               long producerId,\n   550→                                               short producerEpoch,\n   551→             byte magic,\n   558→                                               Compression compression,\n   559→                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n   560→                                               long baseOffset,\n   561→                                               long logAppendTime,\n   562→                                               long       long logAppendTime,\n   577→                                               long producerId,\n   578→                                               short producerEpoch,\n   579→    }\n   587→\n   588→    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(Compression compression, SimpleRecord... records) {\n   589→        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, SimpleRecord... records) {\n   632→        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, initialOffset, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n   633→                producerId, producerEpoch,                                                short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {\n   638→        return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n   639→                producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, true, records);\n   640→    }\n   641→\n   642→    public static compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, producerId, producerEpoch,\n   646→                baseSequence, partitionLeaderEpoch, true, records);\n   647→    }\n   648→\n   649→    public static 655→\n   656→    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n   657→                                            TimestampType timestampType, SimpleRecord... records) {\n   658→        return withRecords(magic, initialOffset, compression, timestampType, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID,\n   659→                RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH,\n   660→                false, records);\n   661→    }\n   662→\n   663→    public static MemoryRecords withRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n   664→                                            TimestampType timestampType, long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n   665→                                            int baseSequence, int partitionLeaderEpoch, boolean isTransactional,\n   666→                                            SimpleRecord... records) {\n   667→        if (records.length == 0)\n   668→            return MemoryRecords.EMPTY;\n   669→        int sizeEstimate = AbstractRecords.estimateSizeInBytes(magic, compression.type(), Arrays.asList(records));\n   670→        ByteBufferOutputStream bufferStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(sizeEstimate);\n   671→        long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n   672→        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n   673→            logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();\n   674→        try (final MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferStream, magic, compression, timestampType,\n   675→                initialOffset, logAppendTime, producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional, false,\n   676→                partitionLeaderEpoch, sizeEstimate)) {\n   677→            for (SimpleRecord record : records)\n   678→                builder.append(record);\n   679→            return builder.build();\n   680→        }\n   681→    }\n   682→\n   683→    public static MemoryRecords withEndTransactionMarker(long producerId, short producerEpoch, EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n   684→        return withEndTransactionMarker(0L, System.currentTimeMillis(), RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH,\n   685→                producerId, producerEpoch, marker);\n   686→    }\n   687→\n   688→    public static MemoryRecords MemoryRecords withEndTransactionMarker(long initialOffset, long timestamp, int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n   695→                                                         long producerId, short  705→    public static void writeEndTransactionalMarker(ByteBuffer buffer, long initialOffset, long timestamp,\n   706→                                                   int partitionLeaderEpoch, long producerId, producerEpoch,\n   711→                RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, isTransactional, true, partitionLeaderEpoch,\n   712→                buffer.capacity())\n   713→        ) {\n   714→            builder.appendEndTxnMarker(timestamp, marker);\n   715→        }\n   716→    }\n   717→\n   718→    public static MemoryRecords withLeaderChangeMessage(\n   719→        long initialOffset,\n   720→ See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.KRaftVersionRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.LeaderChangeMessage;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SnapshotFooterRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.SnapshotHeaderRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.message.VotersRecord;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.network.TransferableChannel;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.MemoryRecords.RecordFilter.BatchRetention;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.MemoryRecords.RecordFilter.BatchRetentionResult;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.AbstractIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteBufferOutputStream;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Utils;\n\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Arrays;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.Objects;\n\n/**\n * A {@link Records} implementation backed by a ByteBuffer. This is used only for reading or\n * modifying in-place an existing buffer of record batches. To create a new buffer see {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder},\n * or one of the {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)} variants.\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {\n    public static final MemoryRecords EMPTY = MemoryRecords.readableRecords(ByteBuffer.allocate(0));\n\n    private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n\n    private final Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches = this::batchIterator;\n\n    private int validBytes = -1;\n\n    // Construct a writable memory records\n    private MemoryRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(buffer, \"buffer should not be null\");\n        this.buffer = buffer;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int sizeInBytes() {\n        return buffer.limit();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int writeTo(TransferableChannel channel, int position, int length) throws IOException {\n        if (((long) position) + length > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"position+length should not be greater than buffer.limit(), position: \"\n                    + position + \", length: \" + length + \", buffer.limit(): \" + buffer.limit());\n\n        return Utils.tryWriteTo(channel, position, length, buffer);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write all records to the given channel (including partial records).\n     * @param channel The channel to write to\n     * @return The number of bytes written\n     * @throws IOException For any IO errors writing to the channel\n     */\n    public int writeFullyTo(GatheringByteChannel channel) throws IOException {\n        buffer.mark();\n        int written = 0;\n        while (written < sizeInBytes())\n            written += channel.write(buffer);\n        buffer.reset();\n        return written;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * The total number of bytes in this message set not including any partial, trailing messages. This\n     * may be smaller than what is returned by {@link #sizeInBytes()}.\n     * @return The number of valid bytes\n     */\n    public int validBytes() {\n        if (validBytes >= 0)\n            return validBytes;\n\n        int bytes = 0;\n        for (RecordBatch batch : batches())\n            bytes += batch.sizeInBytes();\n\n        this.validBytes = bytes;\n        return bytes;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public AbstractIterator<MutableRecordBatch> batchIterator() {\n        return new RecordBatchIterator<>(new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer.duplicate(), Integer.MAX_VALUE));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Validates the header of the first batch and returns batch size.\n     * @return first batch size including LOG_OVERHEAD if buffer contains header up to\n     *         magic byte, null otherwise\n     * @throws CorruptRecordException if record size or magic is invalid\n     */\n    public Integer firstBatchSize() {\n        if (buffer.remaining() < HEADER_SIZE_UP_TO_MAGIC)\n            return null;\n        return new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer, Integer.MAX_VALUE).nextBatchSize();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Filter the records into the provided ByteBuffer.\n     *\n     * @param filter                      The filter function\n     * @param destinationBuffer           The byte buffer to write the filtered records to\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported. For small\n     *                                    record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4) will\n     *                                    dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the\n     *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant\n     *                                    performance impact.\n     * @return A FilterResult with a summary of the output (for metrics) and potentially an overflow buffer\n     */\n    public FilterResult filterTo(RecordFilter filter, ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n        return filterTo(batches(), filter, destinationBuffer, decompressionBufferSupplier);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Note: This method is also used to convert the first timestamp of the batch (which is usually the timestamp of the first record)\n     * to the delete horizon of the tombstones or txn markers which are present in the batch.\n     */\n    private static FilterResult filterTo(Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches, RecordFilter filter,\n                                         ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n        FilterResult filterResult = new FilterResult(destinationBuffer);\n        ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(destinationBuffer);\n        for (MutableRecordBatch batch : batches) {\n            final BatchRetentionResult batchRetentionResult = filter.checkBatchRetention(batch);\n            final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = batchRetentionResult.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            final BatchRetention batchRetention = batchRetentionResult.batchRetention;\n\n            filterResult.bytesRead += batch.sizeInBytes();\n\n            if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.DELETE)\n                continue;\n\n            final BatchFilterResult iterationResult = filterBatch(batch, decompressionBufferSupplier, filterResult,\n                filter);\n            List<Record> retainedRecords = iterationResult.retainedRecords;\n            boolean containsTombstones = iterationResult.containsTombstones;\n            boolean writeOriginalBatch = iterationResult.writeOriginalBatch;\n            long maxOffset = iterationResult.maxOffset;\n\n            if (!retainedRecords.isEmpty()) {\n                // we check if the delete horizon should be set to a new value\n                // in which case, we need to reset the base timestamp and overwrite the timestamp deltas\n                // if the batch does not contain tombstones, then we don't need to overwrite batch\n                boolean needToSetDeleteHorizon = (containsTombstones || containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) &&\n                    batch.deleteHorizonMs().isEmpty();\n                if (writeOriginalBatch && !needToSetDeleteHorizon) {\n                    batch.writeTo(bufferOutputStream);\n                    filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, retainedRecords.size(), false);\n                } else {\n                    long deleteHorizonMs;\n                    if (needToSetDeleteHorizon)\n                        deleteHorizonMs = filter.currentTime + filter.deleteRetentionMs;\n                    else\n                        deleteHorizonMs = batch.deleteHorizonMs().orElse(RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP);\n                    try (final MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = buildRetainedRecordsInto(batch, retainedRecords,\n                            bufferOutputStream, deleteHorizonMs)) {\n                        MemoryRecords records = builder.build();\n                        int filteredBatchSize = records.sizeInBytes();\n                        MemoryRecordsBuilder.RecordsInfo info = builder.info();\n                        filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(info.maxTimestamp, info.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp,\n                            maxOffset, retainedRecords.size(), filteredBatchSize);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.RETAIN_EMPTY) {\n                if (batch.magic() < RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2) // should never happen\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\"Empty batches are only supported for magic v2 and above\");\n\n                bufferOutputStream.ensureRemaining(DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD);\n                DefaultRecordBatch.writeEmptyHeader(bufferOutputStream.buffer(), RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, batch.producerId(),\n                        batch.producerEpoch(), batch.baseSequence(), batch.baseOffset(), batch.lastOffset(),\n                        batch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), batch.timestampType(), batch.maxTimestamp(),\n                        batch.isTransactional(), batch.isControlBatch());\n                filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, 0, true);\n            }\n\n            // If we had to allocate a new buffer to fit the filtered buffer (see KAFKA-5316), return early to\n            // avoid the need for additional allocations.\n            ByteBuffer outputBuffer = bufferOutputStream.buffer();\n            if (outputBuffer != destinationBuffer) {\n                filterResult.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n                return filterResult;\n            }\n        }\n\n        return filterResult;\n    }\n\n    private static BatchFilterResult filterBatch(RecordBatch batch,\n                                                 BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier,\n                                                 FilterResult filterResult,\n                                                 RecordFilter filter) {\n        try (final CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = batch.streamingIterator(decompressionBufferSupplier)) {\n            long maxOffset = -1;\n            boolean containsTombstones = false;\n            // Convert records with old record versions\n            boolean writeOriginalBatch = batch.magic() >= RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE;\n            List<Record> retainedRecords = new ArrayList<>();\n            while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n                Record record = iterator.next();\n                filterResult.messagesRead += 1;\n\n                if (filter.shouldRetainRecord(batch, record)) {\n                    if (record.offset() > maxOffset)\n                        maxOffset = record.offset();\n\n                    retainedRecords.add(record);\n\n                    if (!record.hasValue()) {\n                        containsTombstones = true;\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    writeOriginalBatch = false;\n                }\n            }\n            return new BatchFilterResult(retainedRecords, writeOriginalBatch, containsTombstones, maxOffset);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class BatchFilterResult {\n        private final List<Record> retainedRecords;\n        private final boolean writeOriginalBatch;\n        private final boolean containsTombstones;\n        private final long maxOffset;\n        private BatchFilterResult(List<Record> retainedRecords,\n                                  final boolean writeOriginalBatch,\n                                  final boolean containsTombstones,\n                                  final long maxOffset) {\n            this.retainedRecords = retainedRecords;\n            this.writeOriginalBatch = writeOriginalBatch;\n            this.containsTombstones = containsTombstones;\n            this.maxOffset = maxOffset;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static MemoryRecordsBuilder buildRetainedRecordsInto(RecordBatch originalBatch,\n                                                                 List<Record> retainedRecords,\n                                                                 ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream,\n                                                                 final long deleteHorizonMs) {\n        Compression compression = Compression.of(originalBatch.compressionType()).build();\n        // V0 has no timestamp type or timestamp, so we set the timestamp to CREATE_TIME and timestamp to NO_TIMESTAMP.\n        // Note that this differs from produce up-conversion where the timestamp type topic config is used and the log append\n        // time is generated if the config is LOG_APPEND_TIME. The reason for the different behavior is that there is\n        // no appropriate log append time we can generate at compaction time.\n        TimestampType timestampType = originalBatch.timestampType() == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE ?\n                TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : originalBatch.timestampType();\n        long logAppendTime = timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ?\n                originalBatch.maxTimestamp() : RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        long baseOffset = originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2 ?\n                originalBatch.baseOffset() : retainedRecords.get(0).offset();\n\n        // Convert records with older record versions to the current one\n        MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferOutputStream, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\n                compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime, originalBatch.producerId(),\n                originalBatch.producerEpoch(), originalBatch.baseSequence(), originalBatch.isTransactional(),\n                originalBatch.isControlBatch(), originalBatch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), bufferOutputStream.limit(), deleteHorizonMs);\n\n        for (Record record : retainedRecords)\n            builder.append(record);\n\n        if (originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2)\n            // we must preserve the last offset from the initial batch in order to ensure that the\n            // last sequence number from the batch remains even after compaction. Otherwise, the producer\n            // could incorrectly see an out of sequence error.\n            builder.overrideLastOffset(originalBatch.lastOffset());\n\n        return builder;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get the byte buffer that backs this instance for reading.\n     */\n    public ByteBuffer buffer() {\n        return buffer.duplicate();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public MemoryRecords slice(int position, int size) {\n        if (position < 0)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid position: \" + position + \" in read from \" + this);\n        if (position > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Slice from position \" + position + \" exceeds end position of \" + this);\n        if (size < 0)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid size: \" + size + \" in read from \" + this);\n\n        int availableBytes = Math.min(size, buffer.limit() - position);\n        // As of now, clients module support Java11 hence can't use ByteBuffer::slice(position, size) method.\n        // So we need to create a duplicate buffer and set the position and limit. Duplicate buffer\n        // is backed by original bytes hence not the content but only the relative position and limit\n        // are changed in the duplicate buffer. Once the position and limit are set, we can call the\n        // slice method to get the sliced buffer, which is a backed by the original buffer with the\n        // position reset to 0 and limit set to the size of the slice.\n        ByteBuffer slicedBuffer = buffer.duplicate();\n        slicedBuffer.position(position);\n        slicedBuffer.limit(position + availableBytes);\n        // Reset the position to 0 so that the sliced view has a relative position.\n        slicedBuffer = slicedBuffer.slice();\n\n        return readableRecords(slicedBuffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches() {\n        return batches;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String toString() {\n        return \"MemoryRecords(size=\" + sizeInBytes() +\n                \", buffer=\" + buffer +\n                \")\";\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean equals(Object o) {\n        if (this == o)\n            return true;\n        if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\n            return false;\n\n        MemoryRecords that = (MemoryRecords) o;\n\n        return buffer.equals(that.buffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int hashCode() {\n        return buffer.hashCode();\n    }\n\n    public abstract static class RecordFilter {\n        public final long currentTime;\n        public final long deleteRetentionMs;\n\n        public RecordFilter(final long currentTime, final long deleteRetentionMs) {\n            this.currentTime = currentTime;\n            this.deleteRetentionMs = deleteRetentionMs;\n        }\n\n        public static class BatchRetentionResult {\n            public final BatchRetention batchRetention;\n            public final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            public BatchRetentionResult(final BatchRetention batchRetention,\n                                        final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) {\n                this.batchRetention = batchRetention;\n                this.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            }\n        }\n\n        public enum BatchRetention {\n            DELETE, // Delete the batch without inspecting records\n            RETAIN_EMPTY, // Retain the batch even if it is empty\n            DELETE_EMPTY  // Delete the batch if it is empty\n        }\n\n        /**\n         * Check whether the full batch can be discarded (i.e. whether we even need to\n         * check the records individually).\n         */\n        protected abstract BatchRetentionResult checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch batch);\n\n        /**\n         * Check whether a record should be retained in the log. Note that {@link #checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)}\n         * is used prior to checking individual record retention. Only records from batches which were not\n         * explicitly discarded with {@link BatchRetention#DELETE} will be considered.\n         */\n        protected abstract boolean shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch recordBatch, Record record);\n    }\n\n    public static class FilterResult {\n        private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;\n        private int messagesRead = 0;\n        // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from\n        // `messagesRead` and any discarded batches.\n        private int bytesRead = 0;\n        private int messagesRetained = 0;\n        private int bytesRetained = 0;\n        private long maxOffset = -1L;\n        private long maxTimestamp = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        private long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = -1L;\n\n        private FilterResult(ByteBuffer outputBuffer) {\n            this.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n        }\n\n        private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(MutableRecordBatch retainedBatch, int numMessagesInBatch, boolean headerOnly) {\n            int bytesRetained = headerOnly ? DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD : retainedBatch.sizeInBytes();\n            updateRetainedBatchMetadata(retainedBatch.maxTimestamp(), retainedBatch.lastOffset(),\n                    retainedBatch.lastOffset(), numMessagesInBatch, bytesRetained);\n        }\n\n        private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset,\n                                                 int messagesRetained, int bytesRetained) {\n            validateBatchMetadata(maxTimestamp, shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, maxOffset);\n            if (maxTimestamp > this.maxTimestamp) {\n                this.maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp;\n                this.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\n            }\n            this.maxOffset = Math.max(maxOffset, this.maxOffset);\n            this.messagesRetained += messagesRetained;\n            this.bytesRetained += bytesRetained;\n        }\n\n        private void {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01CUJAvEeMhGtvbuaUd4Zmaa","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01N1XD4BNiXESpQyP2EFN8aF","name":"Edit","input":{"replace_all":false,"file_path":"/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java","old_string":"/**\n * A record batch is a container for records. In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),\n * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain\n * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records\n * regardless of compression.\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {","new_string":"/**\n * A record batch is a container for records in the Kafka log. In old versions of the record format\n * (versions 0 and 1), a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but\n * could contain many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally\n * contain many records regardless of compression.\n *\n * <p>This interface provides access to batch metadata (offsets, timestamps, producer information,\n * compression type) and an iterator over the individual {@link Record}s contained in the batch.\n *\n * <p><b>Thread Safety:</b> Implementations are generally not thread-safe. The calling code must\n * ensure that batches are not accessed concurrently from multiple threads. However, read-only access\n * to immutable batch data is safe.\n *\n * <p><b>Protocol Role:</b> RecordBatch abstracts the Kafka record format (currently magic v0 through v2).\n * It handles serialization and deserialization of record batches in the on-disk and on-wire format,\n * which is critical for:\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Producer clients sending records to brokers</li>\n *   <li>Brokers storing records in the log</li>\n *   <li>Consumer clients reading records from the log</li>\n *   <li>Brokers validating CRC checksums for data integrity</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><b>Compression Support:</b> This interface supports multiple compression types (GZIP, Snappy, LZ4, ZSTD).\n * Decompression is transparent to callers using the {@link #iterator()} or {@link #streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)}.\n *\n * @see {@link DefaultRecordBatch} for the magic v2+ implementation\n * @see {@link Record} for individual records within a batch\n * @see {@link MemoryRecords} for a collection of record batches in memory\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":12,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1183,"cache_read_input_tokens":49006,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1183},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9d9fd0c8-07b2-42f1-8f5f-a72629c84b26","uuid":"cc4e6d6c-3198-407f-abfa-5c4643f4c676"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01N1XD4BNiXESpQyP2EFN8aF","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java has been updated successfully."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9d9fd0c8-07b2-42f1-8f5f-a72629c84b26","uuid":"4209e038-43f6-4c76-af01-ac9ce7ca9b48","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/workspace/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java","oldString":"/**\n * A record batch is a container for records. In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),\n * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain\n * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records\n * regardless of compression.\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {","newString":"/**\n * A record batch is a container for records in the Kafka log. In old versions of the record format\n * (versions 0 and 1), a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but\n * could contain many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally\n * contain many records regardless of compression.\n *\n * <p>This interface provides access to batch metadata (offsets, timestamps, producer information,\n * compression type) and an iterator over the individual {@link Record}s contained in the batch.\n *\n * <p><b>Thread Safety:</b> Implementations are generally not thread-safe. The calling code must\n * ensure that batches are not accessed concurrently from multiple threads. However, read-only access\n * to immutable batch data is safe.\n *\n * <p><b>Protocol Role:</b> RecordBatch abstracts the Kafka record format (currently magic v0 through v2).\n * It handles serialization and deserialization of record batches in the on-disk and on-wire format,\n * which is critical for:\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Producer clients sending records to brokers</li>\n *   <li>Brokers storing records in the log</li>\n *   <li>Consumer clients reading records from the log</li>\n *   <li>Brokers validating CRC checksums for data integrity</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><b>Compression Support:</b> This interface supports multiple compression types (GZIP, Snappy, LZ4, ZSTD).\n * Decompression is transparent to callers using the {@link #iterator()} or {@link #streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)}.\n *\n * @see {@link DefaultRecordBatch} for the magic v2+ implementation\n * @see {@link Record} for individual records within a batch\n * @see {@link MemoryRecords} for a collection of record batches in memory\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {","originalFile":"/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\n\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.util.Iterator;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport java.util.OptionalLong;\n\n/**\n * A record batch is a container for records. In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),\n * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain\n * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records\n * regardless of compression.\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {\n\n    /**\n     * The \"magic\" values\n     */\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V0 = 0;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V1 = 1;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V2 = 2;\n\n    /**\n     * The current \"magic\" value\n     */\n    byte CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE = MAGIC_VALUE_V2;\n\n    /**\n     * Timestamp value for records without a timestamp\n     */\n    long NO_TIMESTAMP = -1L;\n\n    /**\n     * Values used in the v2 record format by non-idempotent/non-transactional producers or when\n     * up-converting from an older format.\n     */\n    long NO_PRODUCER_ID = -1L;\n    short NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH = -1;\n    int NO_SEQUENCE = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Used to indicate an unknown leader epoch, which will be the case when the record set is\n     * first created by the producer.\n     */\n    int NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether the checksum of this batch is correct.\n     *\n     * @return true If so, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Raise an exception if the checksum is not valid.\n     */\n    void ensureValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the checksum of this record batch, which covers the batch header as well as all of the records.\n     *\n     * @return The 4-byte unsigned checksum represented as a long\n     */\n    long checksum();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the max timestamp or log append time of this record batch.\n     *\n     * If the timestamp type is create time, this is the max timestamp among all records contained in this batch and\n     * the value is updated during compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The max timestamp\n     */\n    long maxTimestamp();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the timestamp type of this record batch. This will be {@link TimestampType#NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE}\n     * if the batch has magic 0.\n     *\n     * @return The timestamp type\n     */\n    TimestampType timestampType();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base offset contained in this record batch. For magic version prior to 2, the base offset will\n     * always be the offset of the first message in the batch. This generally requires deep iteration and will\n     * return the offset of the first record in the record batch. For magic version 2 and above, this will return\n     * the first offset of the original record batch (i.e. prior to compaction). For non-compacted topics, the\n     * behavior is equivalent.\n     *\n     * Because this requires deep iteration for older magic versions, this method should be used with\n     * caution. Generally {@link #lastOffset()} is safer since access is efficient for all magic versions.\n     *\n     * @return The base offset of this record batch (which may or may not be the offset of the first record\n     *         as described above).\n     */\n    long baseOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the last offset in this record batch (inclusive). Just like {@link #baseOffset()}, the last offset\n     * always reflects the offset of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log\n     * compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The offset of the last record in this batch\n     */\n    long lastOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the offset following this record batch (i.e. the last offset contained in this batch plus one).\n     *\n     * @return the next consecutive offset following this batch\n     */\n    long nextOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the record format version of this record batch (i.e its magic value).\n     *\n     * @return the magic byte\n     */\n    byte magic();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the producer id for this log record batch. For older magic versions, this will return -1.\n     *\n     * @return The producer id or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    long producerId();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the producer epoch for this log record batch.\n     *\n     * @return The producer epoch, or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    short producerEpoch();\n\n    /**\n     * Does the batch have a valid producer id set.\n     */\n    boolean hasProducerId();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #baseOffset()}, this value is not\n     * affected by compaction: it always retains the base sequence number from the original batch.\n     *\n     * @return The first sequence number or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    int baseSequence();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the last sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #lastOffset()}, the last sequence number\n     * always reflects the sequence number of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log\n     * compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The last sequence number or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    int lastSequence();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the compression type of this record batch.\n     *\n     * @return The compression type\n     */\n    CompressionType compressionType();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the size in bytes of this batch, including the size of the record and the batch overhead.\n     * @return The size in bytes of this batch\n     */\n    int sizeInBytes();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the count if it is efficiently supported by the record format (which is only the case\n     * for magic 2 and higher).\n     *\n     * @return The number of records in the batch or null for magic versions 0 and 1.\n     */\n    Integer countOrNull();\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether this record batch is compressed.\n     * @return true if so, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isCompressed();\n\n    /**\n     * Write this record batch into a buffer.\n     * @param buffer The buffer to write the batch to\n     */\n    void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer);\n\n    /**\n     * Whether or not this record batch is part of a transaction.\n     * @return true if it is, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isTransactional();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the delete horizon, returns OptionalLong.EMPTY if the first timestamp is not the delete horizon\n     * @return timestamp of the delete horizon\n     */\n    OptionalLong deleteHorizonMs();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the partition leader epoch of this record batch.\n     * @return The leader epoch or -1 if it is unknown\n     */\n    int partitionLeaderEpoch();\n\n    /**\n     * Return a streaming iterator which basically delays decompression of the record stream until the records\n     * are actually asked for using {@link Iterator#next()}. If the message format does not support streaming\n     * iteration, then the normal iterator is returned. Either way, callers should ensure that the iterator is closed.\n     *\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported.\n     *                                    For small record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4)\n     *                                    will dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the\n     *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant\n     *                                    performance impact.\n     * @return The closeable iterator\n     */\n    CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier);\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether this is a control batch (i.e. whether the control bit is set in the batch attributes).\n     * For magic versions prior to 2, this is always false.\n     *\n     * @return Whether this is a batch containing control records\n     */\n    boolean isControlBatch();\n\n    /**\n     * iterate all records to find the offset of max timestamp.\n     * noted:\n     * 1) that the earliest offset will return if there are multi records having same (max) timestamp\n     * 2) it always returns None if the {@link RecordBatch#magic()} is equal to {@link RecordBatch#MAGIC_VALUE_V0}\n     * @return offset of max timestamp\n     */\n    default Optional<Long> offsetOfMaxTimestamp() {\n        if (magic() == RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V0) return Optional.empty();\n        long maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp();\n        try (CloseableIterator<Record> iter = streamingIterator(BufferSupplier.create())) {\n            while (iter.hasNext()) {\n                Record record = iter.next();\n                if (maxTimestamp == record.timestamp()) return Optional.of(record.offset());\n            }\n        }\n        return Optional.empty();\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":26,"oldLines":10,"newStart":26,"newLines":34,"lines":[" import java.util.OptionalLong;"," "," /**","- * A record batch is a container for records. It implements the {@link MutableRecordBatch} interface to support\n * in-place modification of batch metadata after creation (e.g., during log compaction or timestamp\n * updates).\n *\n * <p><b>Thread Safety:</b> <b>NOT thread-safe.</b> The internal {@link ByteBuffer} is not\n * synchronized. Callers must ensure that instances are not accessed concurrently from multiple threads.\n * However, methods are safe to call sequentially from a single thread.\n *\n * <p><b>Performance Characteristics:</b>\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Batch header access (magic, compression, offset, etc.) is O(1) and very fast as it reads\n *       directly from the buffer without iteration.</li>\n *   <li>Record iteration is lazy - the {@link #iterator()} method returns an iterator that\n *       only decompresses records as needed.</li>\n *   <li>{@link #streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)} provides memory-efficient decompression\n *       using a supplied buffer, useful for processing very large batches.</li>\n *   <li>{@link #skipKeyValueIterator(BufferSupplier)} is optimized to iterate records while\n *       skipping key/value payload data, useful for log validation.</li>\n *   <li>Checksum validation is performed via {@link #isValid()} and can be deferred until needed.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><b>Log Compaction Considerations:</b> This class preserves the original batch's base offset\n * and sequence numbers even when records are removed during log compaction. It implements the {@link MutableRecordBatch} interface to support\n * in-place modification of batch metadata after creation (e.g., during log compaction or timestamp\n * updates).\n *\n * <p><b>Thread Safety:</b> <b>NOT thread-safe.</b> The internal {@link ByteBuffer} is not\n * synchronized. Callers must ensure that instances are not accessed concurrently from multiple threads.\n * However, methods are safe to call sequentially from a single thread.\n *\n * <p><b>Performance Characteristics:</b>\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Batch header access (magic, compression, offset, etc.) is O(1) and very fast as it reads\n *       directly from the buffer without iteration.</li>\n *   <li>Record iteration is lazy - the {@link #iterator()} method returns an iterator that\n *       only decompresses records as needed.</li>\n *   <li>{@link #streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)} provides memory-efficient decompression\n *       using a supplied buffer, useful for processing very large batches.</li>\n *   <li>{@link #skipKeyValueIterator(BufferSupplier)} is optimized to iterate records while\n *       skipping key/value payload data, useful for log validation.</li>\n *   <li>Checksum validation is performed via {@link #isValid()} and can be deferred until needed.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><b>Log Compaction Considerations:</b> This class preserves the original batch's base offset\n * and sequence numbers even when records are removed during log compaction.
